name: The Langel Firm
description_short: NYC debt defense attorney specializing in defending consumers against collection lawsuits, wage garnishments, and bank restraints under FDCPA regulations.
description_long:
The Langel Firm is a New York City-based law practice founded by Jesse Langel, an attorney focused exclusively on defending consumers and businesses facing debt-collection litigation. The firm has built its reputation on opposing the same debt collectors, banks, and debt buyers repeatedly, developing deep expertise in the procedural and regulatory landscape of debt defense.

The firm offers comprehensive legal defense against debt-collection lawsuits, judgment enforcement actions, and collection abuse. Services include defending against summons and complaints, challenging improper service of papers, protecting exempt income from wage garnishments and bank restraints, negotiating settlements, and litigating cases. The firm also pursues affirmative claims against collectors for Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) violations, including harassment, misrepresentation, improper service, and false credit reporting.

What distinguishes The Langel Firm is its specialized investigation process: verifying debt ownership, scrutinizing all legal documentation for procedural defects, and challenging the admissibility of collector evidence including credit-card agreements and account statements. The firm emphasizes swift action to protect exempt money (unemployment, Social Security, retirement income) while resolving underlying judgments. Jesse Langel has received recognition as a top three consumer lawyer in New York (3BestRated.com), holds an AV® rating from Martindale-Hubbell®, appeared in SuperLawyers magazine, and is rated "Excellent" by Avvo.com.

This is a legitimate debt defense practice, not a debt settlement or credit repair company. However, potential clients should understand that The Langel Firm defends against collection lawsuits rather than providing bankruptcy filing services, despite being categorized under bankruptcy. The firm's approach assumes clients can afford legal representation and focuses on litigation and negotiation rather than debt consolidation or settlement programs.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Specialized expertise in FDCPA violations and collection misconduct—can pursue counterclaims against collectors for harassment, misrepresentation, and improper service
Focuses on protecting exempt income (Social Security, unemployment, retirement) during wage garnishments and bank restraints
Investigates debt ownership and challenges admissibility of collector evidence, including credit-card agreements
Established reputation opposing the same debt collectors repeatedly, providing insight into collector tactics
AV® rated by Martindale-Hubbell® and recognized as top three consumer lawyer in New York
Willing to negotiate or litigate, with explicit focus on client financial security and credit health
Handles judgment reversal and vacation of existing judgments, not just defense of new lawsuits
cons: Limited service scope—defends against lawsuits and enforcement actions but does not offer debt consolidation, settlement, or credit repair services
No pricing information provided on website; legal representation cost may be prohibitive for some consumers
Geographically limited to New York City; not accessible to consumers in other states
No information on contingency arrangements, payment plans, or pro-bono options
Services are litigation-focused; not suitable for consumers seeking to avoid court or resolve debt without legal confrontation
best_for: NYC consumers who have been served with a debt-collection lawsuit and need courtroom defense
Consumers facing wage garnishments or bank restraints who need swift action to protect exempt income
Consumers who believe they have been subject to FDCPA violations and want to pursue counterclaims
Consumers with existing judgments who want to explore reversal or vacation of judgment
diagnosis: The Langel Firm is best for NYC consumers actively defending against collection lawsuits or dealing with wage garnishments and bank restraints who can afford attorney representation. The main caveat is that this firm practices debt defense litigation, not bankruptcy filing; consumers seeking Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 relief should consult a bankruptcy specialist instead.
services: Defense against debt-collection lawsuits and summons
Challenge of improper service of papers and legal documents
Protection and exemption of non-garnishable income
Investigation of debt ownership and chain of title
Challenge to admissibility of collector evidence
Affirmative legal action against debt collectors, law firms, and debt buyers
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 30 Wall St 8th floor, New York, NY 10005
